sections.paragraph:
βραδύς, εῖα, ύ (s. βραδέως; Hom. et al.; SIG 502, 12; Test-Abr A 6 p. 83, 19 [Stone p. 14] τῇ ὀψὲ βραδείᾳ) slow, opp. ταχύς (Ps.-Isocr., ad Demon. 34; Aristot., EN 6, 10, 1142b; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 48) εἰς τὸ λαλῆσαι slow to speak Js 1:19 (B-D-F §402, 2; Rob. 658; βραδὺ φθέγγεσθαι is praised Dio Chrys. 15 [32], 2); β. εἰς ὀργήν ibid. (cp. Dionys. Soph., Ep. 9 β. εἰς ἐπιστολήν; Jos., Ant. 15, 107 βραδὺς εἰς τ. ἀποδόσεις.—Menand., Monost. 99 J. [60 Meineke] πρὸς ὀργὴν β.). Fig., of mental and spiritual slowness (Aristoph., Nub. 129; Polyb. 4, 8, 7; Dion. Hal., Orat. Vet. 2 p. 448; Sext. Emp., Math. 7, 325 w. ἀσύνετος; cp. Il. 10, 226, with a difft. adj.) β. τῇ καρδίᾳ slow of heart=‘dull’, w. inf. foll. (as Thu. 3, 38, 6 προνοῆσαι β.) τοῦ πιστεύειν too dull to believe (B-D-F §400, 8) Lk 24:25. Comp. βραδύτερος (Thu. 4, 8, 1; Theocr. 29, 30; Chion, Ep. 3, 2; Mayser 297): αἴτημα β. λαμβάνειν receive (the answer to) a petition rather slowly Hm 9:7; β. μετανοεῖν be slower in repentance Hs 8, 7, 3; 8, 8, 3. βράδιον (Hes., Op. 528; OGI 502, 17) rather tardy 1 Cl 1:1.—DELG. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Meineke Meineke = AM., ed. Fragmenta comicorum Graecorum—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

difft. difft. = different(ly)

adj. adj. = adjective

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Chion Chion , IV b.c.; for letters attributed to him, I a.d., s. Ep.—List 5

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6
